customTitle: OIDC vs static AWS creds in GitHub Actions

message.content: Is it really worth setting up OIDC for AWS in our GH Actions workflows, or are static creds fine for an internal repo?

content.text: Worth it. Three reasons: (1) static keys live in GitHub Secrets indefinitely — anyone with repo admin can exfiltrate them, and you can't easily detect misuse. OIDC tokens are minted per-job, scoped to a specific role, and expire in an hour. (2) The trust policy ties the role to `repo:owner/repo:ref:refs/heads/main` — a fork or feature branch can't assume the role. (3) Auditing: CloudTrail shows `aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials` per run with the workflow context, which is much better than a long-lived IAM user that shows up in every audit. The setup is ~30 minutes and a one-time cost; the operational savings compound forever.
